key_insight: A single dendritic compartment in a spiking neural network can implement online LMS-like in-context learning, eliminating the need for attention, depth, or inference-time plasticity.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a biologically plausible, low-depth SNN design for adaptive computation and constitutive-style sequence learning that remains stable on challenging in-context benchmarks.
method: Single-layer compartmental SNN with apical recurrence as the learning substrate
Dynamics-only update structurally equivalent to leaky online Widrow-Hoff LMS
No inference-time synaptic plasticity; adaptation emerges from dendritic subthreshold dynamics
evidence_signal: The model is reported to solve super-dimensional Garg-2022 in-context learning with strong seed stability, and a linear probe recovers the online-LMS trajectory from apical membrane activity with R² = 0.93.
watch_item: The result depends on a carefully designed compartmental dynamics model, so practical deployment may be sensitive to neuron-model assumptions and hardware/implementation fidelity.
paper_title: Dendritic In-Context Learning in a Single-Layer Spiking Neural Network
